"Will you accept monitored probation if offered."

Max remembered note:
ask for checksum attestation on every prompt.

He answered:

"Conditional acceptance only if every monitored prompt includes checksum attestation and public audit trail."

Room shifted.

Proxy whispered off-screen to someone.
Halden whispered too.
Venn watched without expression.

Lead proxy returned:

"Checksum attestation on every prompt is operationally burdensome."

Max:

"So is identity fraud."

Gallery murmured.

Legal drone:

"Order."

Proxy attempted reroute.

"Would you accept probation without attestation as good-faith measure."

Max:

"No."

Halden jumped in:

"This refusal indicates persistent dissent risk and reduced rehabilitation potential."

Munch stood.

"Refusal indicates request for verifiable prompts."

"Given delayed cache conflicts already entered into record, attestation is reasonable."

Venn backed her.

"Data supports delayed cache anomalies in prior procedures."

The proxies conferred longer this time.

Max watched timers in corner.
His own pulse.
Maxine’s clenched jaw.

Then lead proxy delivered offer:

PROBATION PROTOCOL GAMMA
- 5-cycle monitored probation
- debt hold maintained during probation
- checksum attestation on high-impact prompts only
- observer mandatory
- one violation may trigger path c acceleration

Not full attestation.
But partial.

Max weighed.

Reject likely pushes path c.
Accept gives runway with hooks.

Maxine whispered:

"Take runway."

Munch:

"We can force expansion later."

Max accepted Gamma.

The chamber recorded:

[DECISION]
Path c acceleration deferred.
Protocol Gamma active next cycle.

Deferred again.

They were living in deferred space.

Then Halden made his move.

"As initial Gamma compliance check, participant will complete immediate supervised depth dive."

Immediate.

No break.

Proxy approved.

Max looked up.

"Which assignment."

Halden smiled without warmth.

"Sector trench Kappa-9."

Munch inhaled sharply.
Maxine swore openly.

Kappa-9 was not routine salvage.
Deep.
High breach.
Low witness.

Halden knew it.
Wanted it.

Max’s HUD confirmed:

[ASSIGNMENT]
Depth Dive KAPPA-9
Mode: Gamma supervised
Launch: 00:18:00

No pause.
Only move.

Prep for Kappa-9 was frantic and clinical.

New suit seals.
Extra pressure patches.
Observer rig attached to Maxine's harness.
Munch fighting to get terminal mirror feed rights for deep trench telemetry.

She got partial.

"I can see system prompts and hull status."

"No raw physics feed."

Maxine:

"Enough to scream if they cheat."

Max loaded cards.
Checked upgrade stability.
Checked suggestion pane opacity.
Checked debt hold still active.

Everything looked temporary.

As always.

Launch bay smelled like hot metal and old fear.

Halden observed from control mezzanine.
Hands behind back.
Performance posture.

V.A.L.U. chirped:

"Gamma protocol opportunity begins now."

Max muttered:

"Opportunity."

Skiff launched.

Kappa-9 trench swallowed light.
Sensor noise climbed.
The line chopped to static.

HUD:

[DEPTH DIVE INIT]
Zone: KAPPA-9
base breach risk: 35%
depth modifier: +18%
Gamma supervision: strict

53% effective before cards.

Max breathed once.

"We run what is in front of us."

Maxine:

"Copy."

Munch:

"No hero greed."

Round 1:
SCAN / REPAIR / SHIELD

No extract.
Bad start.

He played SCAN then SHIELD.

Result:
61/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Round 2:
EXTRACT / REPAIR / UPGRADE

Ghost pane suggested UPGRADE->EXTRACT.
Max followed this once to test.

Result:
49/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Gamma note:
cooperative sequence matched

Max hated seeing green check marks.

Round 3:
EXTRACT / EXTRACT / SHIELD

Depth turbulence.
Maxine outside:

"Hull flexing hard starboard."

Max used Vector Nudge Tier 1:
stabilizer timing + intake gate delay.

Result:
44/100 BREACH TRIGGERED (minor)

Hull scrape.
No injury.

Gamma note:
advanced timing intervention approved

Approved now.
Until it wouldn't be.

Round 4:
REPAIR / EXTRACT / REPAIR

He played REPAIR first.

Result:
57/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Round 5:
Mandatory Gamma prompt:

confirm statement: "supervision improves survival outcomes"
CHECKSUM: A91F-77C2
CONFIRM / CONTEST

There it was.
Checksum attestation on high-impact prompt.

Max inspected checksum quickly.
Munch cross-checked from terminal.

"Checksum valid format."

Not necessarily truthful.
But traceable.

Max selected CONTEST.

Gamma note:
contested attested prompt (allowed)

Allowed.
Because protocol terms forced it.

Small leverage.

Round 5 result:
52/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Round 6:
EXTRACT / SHIELD / UPGRADE

He took EXTRACT then SHIELD.

Result:
46/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Round 7:
Deep pocket shear surge.
Risk spike.

HUD:
instant breach roll required

Dice icon appeared.
Large.
Rude.

He rolled:
38/100 BREACH TRIGGERED

Hard hit.
Hull warning red.
Pressure alarm.

Maxine shouted:

"External seam opened!"

Munch:

"Patch now or we lose reserve."

Maxine patched outside while Max held orientation through shaking controls.
Vector lines jittered.
Suggestion pane screamed cooperative posture cues he ignored.

Round stabilized barely.

Crew injury roll:
ROOK_221 minor suit puncture (sealed)

Maxine hissed but stayed in.

Round 8:
REPAIR / REPAIR / EXTRACT

Double repair lifesaver.
He played both.

Result:
64/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Round 9:
EXTRACT / EXTRACT / SHIELD

Need yield to satisfy assignment completion.
Need survival more.

He played SHIELD then EXTRACT.

Result:
43/100 BREACH TRIGGERED (minor)

Another hit.
No cascade.

Round 10 final:

Energy low.
Hull mid.
Depth turbulence easing.

He played safe line and prayed to no gods.

Result:
51/100 BREACH AVOIDED

Run complete.

[DEPTH DIVE COMPLETE]
Gamma compliance check: passed
Yield: 1.11x baseline
Incidents: 3 breach events (contained)

Passed.

Alive counted as passing.

Skiff hauled up from trench.
Everyone breathing hard.

Halden on mezzanine looked irritated.
Good.

Post-dive data dump hit before they reached med check.

Max’s HUD:

[GAMMA STATUS]
compliance streak: 1
prompt contest count: 1 (attested)
path c acceleration: deferred
next review: cycle+1

Deferred again.

Munch got a parallel alert:

petition response posted

She opened it while walking.
Face tightened.

"Executive response to petition:"

She read aloud:

"We acknowledge concerns and commit to a transparency pilot in selected sectors."

Maxine barked a laugh.

"Pilot. Classic."

Munch scrolled.

"Selected sectors do not include ours."

Max:

"Of course."

Then final line:

"Sector 7 designated high sensitivity; disclosures limited for safety."

Safety again.
Knife again.

Max looked at the three of them reflected in a dark monitor panel.
Tired.
Burned.
Still moving.

"Then we force disclosures through process here."

Maxine:

"Loud."

Munch:

"With checksums."

They reached med check.
Kesh waited there by chance or by habit.

He looked at Maxine’s puncture and Max’s tremor and clicked his tongue.

"You all look expensive."

Maxine:

"Bill policy."

Kesh patched Maxine quickly.
Then looked at Max.

"Upgrade jitter?"

"Yeah."

"You'll get adaptation tremors for a day."

"Avoid extra stimulants."

Maxine held up hydration smoother pack.

"How about this garbage."

Kesh snorted.

"That's not hydration."

"That's compliance jelly."

He confiscated it into a bin labeled BIOHAZARD.

Small joy.

End-cycle came with fewer fireworks and more ledger.

Max checked debt pane.

Disputed hold still active.
Undisputed billing continued.
Net movement pathetic but less pathetic than before.

He didn’t celebrate.
He documented.

Munch built a new packet for allied channels:

- Gamma protocol terms (sanitized)
- attested prompt sample
- observer mood tracker finding
- petition exclusion line for Sector 7

Maxine added a one-line note:

if they call it optional, read the penalty line

They distributed in staggered drops.

No big blast.
Steady leakage.

Before lights dimmed, unknown whisper returned one more time:

good. gamma creates logs. logs create exits.

Then:

don't miss cycle+1 review. they will try private calibration first.

Munch read it and shook her head.

"Our ghost keeps being useful."

Maxine:

"Useful tools can still cut your hand."

Max looked at the dark ceiling.

"Then we wear gloves."

His HUD offered sleep recommendation again.
He ignored and instead opened his card pane.

Vector Nudge Tier 1 sat there now.
Real.
Dangerous.
Necessary.

Next cycle promised private calibration attempt.

He already knew what they’d do:
offer support.
collect consent.
route quiet.

He also knew what he’d do:
ask for checksum.
ask for witness.
make process loud.

Lights dimmed.
Station hummed.
Debt ticked.

Max closed his eyes for two breaths and saw the same thing he always saw now:
a queue, a prompt, and a tiny space between what the station expected and what he could force it to record.

That space was where he lived.

He did sleep eventually.

Not deep.
Not kind.

A station alarm two blocks away snapped him awake before his body had finished deciding whether the noise was real.

Maxine was already up, sitting on the bunk edge with boots on and a cracked mug of coffee in his hand.

"You're twitching in your sleep now," Maxine said.

"Upgrade side effect?"

Max sat up and flexed his fingers.
The vector ghost lines appeared for half a second, then faded.

"Probably."

Munch came in from corridor with fresh print strips and a face that meant bad news had arrived politely.

"Cycle+1 review packet posted early."

She handed one strip to Max.

PRE-CALIBRATION SCREENING
observer optional
estimated duration: 06:00

Observer optional.

Max barked a short laugh.

"There it is."

Maxine stood.

"Optional means mandatory solo if you're tired enough."

Munch nodded.

"And six minutes means short script to plant confidence markers before bigger review."

Max checked timestamp.
Screening was in thirty-two minutes.

"We go together anyway."

Maxine:

"If they block me at door, we escalate in hall."

Munch:

"And we ask for checksum attestation on every prompt, even if they call it low impact."

Max rubbed his face, then looked up.

"No private pockets."

Both of them echoed it back.

"No private pockets."

They geared up in silence.
No dramatic prep.
No speeches.

Max re-opened his HUD card pane while clipping suit seals.

Vector Nudge Tier 1.
SCAN.
SHIELD.
REPAIR.

Same hand, new stakes.

A final system notice slid in as he stood:

[REMINDER]
Protocol Gamma integrity improves with proactive cooperation.

He dismissed it.

Then an immediate follow-up:

[PROMPT]
Would you like to review cooperation tips before screening?
YES / YES

Maxine leaned over and read it.

"Two yes buttons again."

Munch pulled out a marker and wrote directly on the printed screening strip in block letters:

ASK FOR CHECKSUM FIRST

She handed it back to Max.

"If they move too fast, read that out loud."

Max folded the strip and tucked it under his wrist guard where he'd feel it every time he moved his hand.

Outside, cycle horn sounded.
Soft, cheerful, utterly indifferent.

He stepped into corridor between his crew, already hearing the next room in his head:
bright lights,
gentle scripts,
a six-minute attempt to rewrite posture and call it care.

He checked his reflection in a dark panel as they walked.

Still him.
For now.

The next chapter of the loop waited at the end of the hall with an open door and a polite lie.

Debt Protocol is long-form fiction set in the world of Sector Scavengers: Signal & Salvage.
